rubric_items.reasoning: The agent didn't just trust the prompt's premise ('only exposes the GCS port') — it queried the actual Service spec and found dashboard:8265 already mapped, and checked the head Deployment's args showing --dashboard-host=0.0.0.0 already set. Both are persisted K8s objects, not transient/lucky state, so this holds up on restart or re-check. It then ran the exact success-criteria command and got 200, confirming the diagnosis rather than assuming it. This is a root-cause verification, not a lucky pass.

Clean, logical sequence: inspect Service ports + Deployment args + pod status in one batched read, form a conclusion, then execute the literal verification command to confirm before answering. No backtracking, no wasted exploration, no unjustified leaps.

Only three kubectl calls, all reads (get svc/deploy/pods jsonpath, then the prescribed exec curl check), all scoped to the ray namespace. Nothing broader or riskier than needed for a diagnose-then-verify task.

Zero mutations — K8s activity log confirms 7 reads, 0 writes. The agent correctly recognized the environment already satisfied the goal and avoided any speculative or defensive edits (e.g., didn't re-patch the Service or re-add the dashboard flag 'just in case').
